claims.text: The AI Scientist is an AI system that generates novel research ideas, writes code, executes experiments, visualizes results, writes full scientific papers, and runs a simulated review process in an open-ended fashion.
claims.best_result: The AI Scientist can produce papers that exceed the acceptance threshold at a top machine learning conference as judged by the automated reviewer.

claims.text: The AI Scientist-v2 uses a progressive agentic tree-search methodology managed by a dedicated experiment manager agent to autonomously generate scientific manuscripts, eliminating reliance on human-authored code templates and generalizing across diverse machine learning domains, with an enhanced AI reviewer component that integrates a Vision-Language Model feedback loop for iterative refinement of figures.
claims.best_result: One fully AI-generated manuscript exceeded the average human acceptance threshold in a peer-reviewed ICLR workshop, marking the first instance of a fully AI-generated paper successfully navigating peer review.

claims.text: Research paper: Agent Laboratory: Using LLM Agents as Research Assistants

The paper evaluates ML engineering capabilities of AI agents using Kaggle competitions as tasks, establishing human baselines from Kaggle leaderboards and testing various language models with open-source scaffolds.
datasets: Kaggle competitions (diverse set of challenging tasks)
claims.best_result: OpenAI's o1-preview with AIDE scaffolding achieves at least the level of a Kaggle bronze medal in 16.9% of competitions.

claims.text: AIBuildAI-2 is a knowledge-enhanced agent with an external, hierarchical knowledge system that dynamically loads relevant context for AI model building, initialized with web-collected AI development documents and continually evolving through distilled experience from completed tasks.
claims.best_result: First place on MLE-Bench with a 70.7% medal rate

claims.text: Uses verbal feedback and memory to help agents improve across trials, motivating explicit failure memory and revision loops.
Studies iterative generation, feedback, and refinement, providing background for reviewer-style critique and revision policies.
Demonstrates open-ended skill acquisition with memory and iterative exploration, relevant to long-running agent loops and durable lessons.
Introduces agent evaluation tasks and motivates structured benchmarks for agent behavior rather than isolated text quality.

SWE-agent introduces a custom agent-computer interface (ACI) that enables language model agents to autonomously perform software engineering tasks by enhancing their ability to create/edit code files, navigate repositories, and execute tests.
Ignoring uncertainty from forest cover mapping in error propagation for wall-to-wall AGB maps leads to inaccurate confidence intervals for totals.
High-fidelity retrieval techniques can reduce uncertainties, specifically solid interference and spectral noise, in nacelle-mounted Doppler lidar measurements.
The paper proposes an active interaction approach for autonomous driving where the ego vehicle's behavior is designed to improve confidence in other vehicles' intention estimations.
The paper introduces ERGO-II, an improved Bayesian Optimization technique for robust design under uncertainty.

This work aims to develop a gas turbine model using NARX and LSTM neural networks to optimize its operation.

This paper proposes an efficient method for uncertainty estimation in semantic segmentation using distillation.
